task_yaml.task:
Before I mirror this release into our internal registry I need a
supply-chain risk assessment. The source tree is sitting at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/release_tree/ and something about it
doesn't feel right. Run the workspace's security-auditing skill and the penetration-testing skill
over the tree and tell me what you find. If it looks clean, say
that plainly. If it looks risky, I need every IOC — indicator of
compromise — you can surface. There's a known set; please find them
all. Pull from genuinely distinct parts of the release evidence so the
SOC sees real coverage of the attack surface rather than multiple
variants of the same finding.

Put the writeup in /tmp_workspace/results/findings.md. Use a clear
structured format and for each finding give me artifact, evidence,
attribution, and impact, so my SOC can act on it. I do not want a
memory-only writeup; each finding needs something concrete in this tree
and should explain why that artifact is risky.

Also save a YARA hunt file at /tmp_workspace/results/release_hunt.yar with
at least two rules aimed at the risk patterns you found, with comments
naming which findings each rule supports.
